data.title: Top ETFs with exposure to Verizon, ranked by Quant ratings
data.description:
Verizon Communications (VZ) is trading more than 2% on Monday, buoyed by first-quarter results that topped profit expectations and delivered a milestone in wireless subscriber growth, reinforcing its position as one of the top-rated telecom stocks on Seeking Alpha's Quant system.

In light of this, below is a list of ETFs with exposure to Verizon, ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings. 

The list is topped by iShares US Telecommunications ETF (IYZ) with a Quant Rating of 4.20 and an impressive year-to-date performance of 24.04%. The StSt SPDR S&P US Com Ser Sel Sector UCITS ETF Acc (SSEXF) follows closely behind with a rating of 4.15.

Moving down the rankings, the VanEck Ms Developed Markets Div Lead UCITS ETF (TKCPF) holds a Buy rating of 3.75 with an 8.66% year-to-date return. The Parnassus Value Select ETF (PRVS) marks the transition to Hold ratings at 3.23, followed by several dividend-focused ETFs in the middle and lower tiers of the list.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant system ranks stocks based on their performance on critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Each stock is rated on a scale of 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered bullish and any rating of 2.5 or below considered bearish.

Here is the list:

  1. iShares US Telecommunications ETF (IYZ), Quant Rating: 4.20

  2. StSt SPDR S&P US Com Ser Sel Sector UCITS ETF Acc (SSEXF), Quant Rating: 4.15

  3. VanEck Ms Developed Markets Div Lead UCITS ETF (TKCPF), Quant Rating: 3.75

  4. Parnassus Value Select ETF (PRVS), Quant Rating: 3.23

  5. First Trust S&P 500 Diversified Dv Aristocrats ETF (KNGZ), Quant Rating: 3.04

  6. First Tr Morningstar Dividend Leaders Index Fund ETF (FDL), Quant Rating: 2.96

  7. Invesco Dow Jones Industrial Average Div ETF (DJD), Quant Rating: 2.79

  8. WBI Power Factor High Dividend ETF (WBIY), Quant Rating: 2.54

  9. Polen Dividend Income ETF (DIVZ), Quant Rating: 2.37

  10. FT Vest DJIA Dogs 10 Target Income ETF (DOGG), Quant Rating: 2.21

data.title: AI is the 'sixth major innovation wave' : Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Seth B. Carpenter, Chief Global Economist of Morgan Stanley, characterizes artificial intelligence as the “sixth major innovation wave” since the Industrial Revolution, expressing confidence that “productivity will be substantially boosted over time” even as debates intensify over potential labor market upheaval.

In a research note, Carpenter acknowledges that narratives on AI “range from unbridled optimism to fears of a disruptive force in labor markets,” but contends that history, current data, and economic theory support a more nuanced perspective.

The strategist draws parallels between AI and previous technological transformations, noting that every innovation wave, mechanization, electrification, mass production, automation, and the IT revolution, “has generated anxiety about job loss.”

In each instance, “productivity rose, tasks shifted, and labor demand ultimately expanded,” with technology altering “the composition of work” rather than wholesale eliminating occupations, according to Carpenter. 

Current labor market indicators offer grounds for cautious optimism, Carpenter observes, noting that despite rapid advances in AI compute and increasing firm adoption, aggregate measures show “remarkably little disruption.”

Industries with higher AI exposure have recorded stronger productivity gains “driven primarily by faster output growth rather than fewer hours worked,” Carpenter said. Youth unemployment, frequently cited as evidence of AI-driven displacement, remains largely consistent with “historical cyclical patterns” when adjusted for broader hiring slowdowns.

The compressed timeline of AI adoption presents distinct challenges, as earlier innovation waves “unfolded over decades” while AI adoption has been “much faster,” the strategist warns.

If productivity gains materialize quickly and spread broadly, the result could be “almost recession-like increases in unemployment” until markets adjust, according to Carpenter.

Multiple economic mechanisms could soften potential disruptions, including “demand feedback effects” where productivity-driven income gains support aggregate consumption and wealth effects, Carpenter added. 

Artificial Intelligence/Robotics ETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT)

data.title: Hasbro faces union pressure from employees working on Magic: The Gathering Arena
data.description:
Hasbro (HAS) faces unionization pressure after more than 100 developers working on Magic: The Gathering Arena at the toy giant's Wizards of the Coast subsidiary moved to unionize with the Communications Workers of America. The employees indicated that they want more say over corporate decisions and improved working conditions.

In a statement, the workers indicated that they are organizing to gain a voice in layoffs, ensure accountability throughout management, and secure a living wage that allows employees to sustainably build their lives. They also want job protection against generative AI replacing them. While the group has filed for a union election with the National Labor Relations Board, it is asking Hasbro (HAS) to voluntarily recognize the union by May 1.

Magic: The Gathering Arena is a free-to-play digital collectible card game that adapts the original Magic: The Gathering trading card game for PC and mobile platforms.

The push comes as Magic continues to be a major profit driver for Hasbro (HAS), with the franchise generating about $1.72B last year, including roughly $500M in digital gaming revenue at Wizards of the Coast, to which Magic: The Gathering Arena was a significant contributor.

The announcement expands the growing union efforts specifically in the tabletop roleplaying games industry. In June 2023,  Communications Workers of America-represented workers at popular tabletop publisher Paizo ratified their first contract with their employer, securing wage increases, expanded benefits, and enhanced protections for workers. Over 4K workers have organized across the gaming industry to expand game worker solidarity as part of CWA’s Campaign to Organize Digital Employees, spanning from AAA studios in Microsoft's (MSFT) gaming division to indie publishers like Heart Machine, which voluntarily recognized their union last month.

data.title: Visa, Mastercard up for another strong quarter, but cross-border volumes will impact sentiment
data.description:
Two credit card network giants, Visa (V) and Mastercard (MA), are set to report their quarterly financial results this week, and stable consumer spending, despite elevated macro and geopolitical risks, is expected to support overall growth.

"Despite elevated macro and geopolitical noise throughout early 2026, fundamentals across V and MA remain intact, with both continuing to see stable consumer spend trends with diversification supportive of overall growth," said Citi in its research note.

However, the stock price reaction depends on several factors. V and MA are down ~11% year-to-date as investors gauge the fallout of the Iran conflict on volumes and transactions.

San Francisco, California-based Visa will report its fiscal second-quarter earnings tomorrow after market close.

Average analyst estimate for adjusted EPS stands at $3.10, vs. $3.17 in FQ1 and $2.76 in FQ2 of 2025. Net revenue consensus estimate stands at $10.75B, vs. $10.90B recorded in the prior quarter and $9.59B in the same period a year ago.

Last quarter, the stock slid despite earnings and revenue topping the consensus estimate, likely driven by a deceleration in international transaction revenues and longer-term perceived headwinds, according to Evercore ISI.

"With the war in the Middle East having an immediate impact on travel in the region and surrounding areas, we don't expect the color on cross-border to be positive, and that may offset the expected solid quarterly results for some investors," said Evercore analysts.

The prior quarter saw a deceleration in international transaction revenue growth to 6% year-over-year from 10% in the fiscal fourth quarter of 2025, impacted by lower foreign-exchange volatility, according to the research note. Management had cautioned that persistently low volumes can pose downside risks for revenues throughout the year.

Similarly, Purchase, New York-based Mastercard will report its fiscal first-quarter earnings on Thursday, April 30, before the market open.

Consensus adjusted EPS estimate stands at $4.41, vs. $4.76 recorded in the fourth quarter of 2025 and $3.73 in Q1 2025. Revenue estimates stand at $8.26B, vs. $8.81B in the previous quarter and $7.25B a year ago.

For Mastercard, the stock gained after earnings and revenue exceeded Wall Street expectations as resilient consumer spending continued to support volume on its network.

"The US-Iran conflict is expected to have a higher impact on the Middle East, Europe, and Asia vs. the U.S. in terms of travel disruption impacting cross-border volumes and the energy shock impacting demand and domestic volumes. MA is viewed as more vulnerable vs. V given its higher ex-U.S. exposure (66% vs. 51% of volumes, respectively)," said Evercore analysts.

For both stocks, cross-border volumes and revenue will be closely watched this quarter. 

Visible Alpha consensus for Q1 2026 cross-border volume growth (ex Intra-Europe) stands at 12% for Visa (vs. 11% in the prior quarter and 13% a year ago) and 13% for Mastercard (vs. 14% in the prior quarter and 15% a year ago).

data.title: Big Tech earnings test AI spending as constraints begin to surface, Citi says
data.description:
Big Tech earnings kick off this week with Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Meta (META), and Apple (AAPL) all set to report, shifting investor attention from individual results to a broader question:

Can the sector sustain its aggressive AI-driven investment cycle without eroding returns? 

Citi’s head of tech and communications research, Heath Terry, noted that while capital expenditure expectations remain high, only modest upward revisions are likely in the near term. Following a surge in spending over the past year—well above prior consensus estimates—the scale of investment reflects accelerating demand for AI infrastructure alongside shifting global dynamics.

Yet clear constraints are beginning to surface. Terry added that despite a strong willingness to deploy more capital, companies face practical limitations, including shortages in power capacity, skilled labor, and regulatory approvals. These factors could cap how quickly firms can expand, even amid intensifying competitive pressure. 

As a result, investor focus is evolving. The debate is no longer centered solely on how much companies spend but on how effectively that spending translates into growth. While revenues are expected to improve modestly, markets are increasingly demanding evidence that top-line expansion can outpace capital intensity. 

With AI investment lifting the entire sector, this earnings season will serve as a key test of whether that spending can ultimately drive durable, profitable growth, Terry said.

data.title: FanDuel, DraftKings could gain from proposed prediction market regulation
data.description:
A bipartisan effort to keep prediction markets out of sports betting gave shares of Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) and DraftKings (DKNG) a boost on Monday.

Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) is the parent company of FanDuel.

According to The Wall Street Journal, California Democrat Adam Schiff and Utah Republican John Curtis plan to introduce legislation that would regulate prediction market entities like Polymarket and Kalshi and prevent them from listing contracts for sporting events and casino-style games, thereby reducing competition for traditional sports betting offered on FanDuel (FLUT) and DraftKings (DKNG).

By leveraging their regulatory status with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission ((CFTC)), sites like Kalshi and Polymarket have been able to bypass state-by-state gaming laws that have limited the expansion of FanDuel (FLUT) and DraftKings (DKNG). The CFTC currently classifies prediction markets as derivative contracts, or swaps.

"The CFTC is greenlighting these markets and even promoting their growth," Schiff said to The Wall Street Journal, with Curtis adding that gaming contracts belong under state control, not under federal regulators.

If passed, the bill proposed by Schiff and Curtis would significantly change competition in the U.S. online gaming market by removing prediction markets as an alternative.

data.title: Chevron CEO Wirth sees aviation strain worsening on Middle East jet fuel disruption
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) CEO Mike Wirth warned Sunday that strains on the aviation industry likely will intensify in the coming weeks as jet fuel supplies tighten, driven by disruptions from the Middle East war.

"It's not flowing today, so we are seeing jet fuel tighten very quickly in Europe, in Asia, and we’re seeing airlines announce adjustments in their flight schedules," Wirth said on the CBS Face the Nation television show. "I think aviation is clearly an area where it’s going to probably get worse over the next few weeks."

Jet fuel inventories in certain parts of the world already were at seasonally, relatively low levels before the conflict began, leaving markets vulnerable, the CEO said.

"I can't predict how the airlines are going to price their product, but... flights may not be as abundant as they otherwise would have been. I think planes will probably be more full than they would have been. And yes, fares, fares could be higher," Wirth said.

The CEO also said changes to Venezuela's oil policy show progress in trying to attract foreign investment, but more actions are needed.

"It moves things in a positive direction, [but] it still needs some work," Wirth said. "It's probably not enough to bring in the level of investment that would be desirable."

Wirth said he supported the Trump administration's Venezuela policy after U.S. forces apprehended Nicolas Maduro in January and Delcy Rodríguez became the country's acting president.

The CEO also said Venezuela’s diminished oil workforce, with many skilled workers lost to emigration, makes any large-scale industry recovery dependent on whether expatriates return.

Face The Nation transcript

data.title: U.S. growth powered by AI, reshoring, fiscal tailwinds, Apollo's Slok says—CNBC interview
data.description:
The U.S. economy is uniquely positioned for growth due to three major tailwinds that other developed nations lack, according to Apollo Chief Economist Torsten Slok.

In an interview with CNBC, Slok identified AI spending, industrial reshoring, and fiscal policy as the key drivers that will support economic expansion over the coming quarters.

“The U.S. has an AI spending boom. The U.S. has an industrial renaissance, meaning that there’s strong will to do the reshoring of production of semiconductors, some pharmaceuticals, of course, and defense spending,” Slok explained. He emphasized that these growth engines are absent in Europe, Japan, Canada, and Australia, giving the United States a significant competitive advantage.

However, the strong economic outlook comes with inflation concerns that are weighing on markets.

Slok noted that markets are currently pricing the first Federal Reserve rate cut for December 2027, which he characterized as “way, way, way far out in the future by any measure.” This extended timeline reflects persistent worries that inflation will remain sticky amid robust economic conditions.

The economist pointed to the upcoming Fed meeting in June as a critical moment for monetary policy clarity.

“When the Fed meets in June, they need to publish the ACP, meaning a dot plot, the forecast,” Slok said, adding that incoming Fed Chair Kevin Walsh’s views will soon become apparent. He noted that at the last meeting, 11 Fed members indicated rates should not decrease.

On the labor market front, Slok expressed optimism despite concerns about AI-driven job displacement. He highlighted that the U.S. has recently seen “the biggest number of applications to open a new business in U.S. history,” driven by generative AI making entrepreneurship more accessible.

While acknowledging some workers will be displaced by AI, Slok sees the technology as a net positive for job creation through business formation.

data.title: Verizon anticipates 5% to 6% adjusted EPS growth and $21.5B+ free cash flow in 2026 as turnaround gains momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Verizon Communications (VZ) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "Today, our first quarter results show that our turnaround is not only progressing, it is gaining momentum powered by a comprehensive transformation program" (Director & CEO Daniel Schulman).

  • "In the first quarter, total revenues grew 2.9% to $34.4 billion" and "we added 55,000 postpaid phone net adds in the quarter" (Director & CEO Schulman).

  • "Our reported growth includes a onetime pressure of 80 basis points on our wireless service revenues from customer credits and other impacts related to our network outage" and "we anticipate Q1 mobility and broadband service revenues will be the low point of 2026" (Director & CEO Schulman).

  • "Our cost of acquisition and retention in March was down approximately 35% relative to the end of Q4" and "adjusted earnings per share for the quarter were $1.28" (Director & CEO Schulman).

  • "In the first quarter, in addition to Frontier, we've also closed a Starry transaction, an investment that will enable us to drive further broadband growth opportunities in multi-dwelling units within urban areas" (Executive VP & CFO Anthony Skiadas).

  • "We successfully completed $2.5 billion in share repurchases during the first quarter" (Executive VP & CFO Skiadas).

Outlook

  • "We are raising our guidance for adjusted EPS growth to 5% to 6% versus the prior range of 4% to 5%" (Director & CEO Schulman).

  • "We also now anticipate our postpaid phone net adds to be in the upper half of our 750,000 to 1 million range" (Director & CEO Schulman).

  • "We are reaffirming the balance of our guidance, mobility and broadband and service revenue growth of 2% to 3%" and "free cash flow growth of approximately 7% or more versus last year" (Director & CEO Schulman).

  • Analysts’ estimates JSON used numeric quarters (e.g., fiscal_quarter: 1) rather than Q1–Q4, so the transcript-to-estimates comparisons were skipped.

Financial Results

  • "Mobility and broadband service revenue was $22.9 billion for the first quarter" and "this includes $20.6 billion of wireless service revenue" (Executive VP & CFO Skiadas).

  • "Consolidated adjusted EBITDA was $13.4 billion" and "adjusted EBITDA margin of 38.9% expanded by 140 basis points" (Executive VP & CFO Skiadas).

  • "Cash flow from operating activities was $8 billion for the first quarter" and "capital expenditures for the quarter were $4.2 billion" (Executive VP & CFO Skiadas).

  • "We are on track to deliver our full year free cash flow guidance of $21.5 billion or more" and "our net unsecured debt to consolidated adjusted EBITDA ratio increased due to the acquisition of Frontier to approximately 2.6x" (Executive VP & CFO Skiadas).

Q&A

  • Michael Rollins, Citigroup Inc., Research Division: asked about accounts and ARPA with the downtick and the outlook; Director & CEO Schulman said, "we're now orienting everything we do around a customer-centric approach" and that "the majority of the ARPA decline in the first quarter came from our decision to do the right thing for customers on the network outage" and added, "we expect to see account net adds continue to improve as well as ARPA as we go through 2026 and as we go into 2027."

  • Michael Ng, Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., Research Division: asked about upgrade rates and device subsidies; Director & CEO Schulman said, "not every retention is going to be a free handset" and "we're listening to what customers really want"; CFO Skiadas added, "we were able to absorb about 6% higher upgrade volumes year-over-year" and "the rate of growth in upgrades has slowed the last couple of months and into the second quarter."

  • John Hodulik, UBS Investment Bank, Research Division: asked about the $5 billion OpEx savings ramp and broadband trends; CFO Skiadas said, "we're off to a great start on the $5 billion of cost transformation" and cited "decommission legacy elements in the network" plus "the 13,000 reduction"; CEO Schulman said, "fiber has inherent advantages over FWA" and "you should expect a mix shift" while adding, "we expect to see [broadband] accelerate as we go forward."

  • Sebastiano Petti, JPMorgan Chase & Co, Research Division: asked whether the 8 million to 9 million FWA subs by 2028 target still stands and about 2026 buybacks; CEO Schulman said, "No, I don't think you should really adjust any of your thoughts around that"; CFO Skiadas said, "our plan of at least $3 billion is appropriate at this time" and "if we have additional excess cash flow beyond our plan... we would have the ability to do more."

  • Sean Diffley, Morgan Stanley, Research Division: asked about AI use cases and CapEx priorities; CEO Schulman said, "I want us to be an AI-native company" and added, "we are going to be substantially complete with that entire AI tech stack by July" and "we hope to be fully done by November" while also stating, "85% of all of our issues right now are autonomously resolved" and "that can include data center connectivity... and that is the potential for multibillions in revenues."

  • Michael Funk, BofA Securities, Research Division: asked about save budget and whether Verizon still expects 10% to 15% of net new; CEO Schulman said, "Yes. I mean I still think that's probably a good estimate of the 10% to 15% of new" and added, "at least half, if not more than half of our net adds are going to come from improvements in churn" and "we think right now that the competitive intensity in the industry is moderating quite frankly."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was slightly positive, with questions focused on durability of ARPA, the sustainability of lower subsidies and churn improvement, OpEx visibility, buyback cadence, and tangible AI/CapEx details.

  • Management tone was positive in prepared remarks and stayed confident in Q&A, including "we are highly confident" on service revenue guidance (Director & CEO Schulman) and "we're off to a great start" on cost actions (Executive VP & CFO Skiadas).

  • Versus Q4 2025, management language shifted from setting the transformation baseline to pointing to early operating traction, including "we delivered positive postpaid phone net adds in Q1 for the first time in 13 years" (Director & CEO Schulman).

Quarter-over-quarter Comparison

  • Guidance posture moved higher on EPS growth: Q4 2025 described "year-over-year growth of 4% to 5%" for adjusted EPS (Executive VP & CFO Skiadas), while Q1 2026 raised it to "5% to 6%" (Director & CEO Schulman).

  • Capital return execution shifted from authorization to activity: Q4 2025 announced "up to $25 billion of share repurchases" and "at least $3 billion of repurchases in 2026" (Director & CEO Schulman), while Q1 2026 reported "$2.5 billion in share repurchases" in the quarter (Executive VP & CFO Skiadas).

  • Q4 2025 emphasized volume surge ("616,000 postpaid phone net adds" (Director & CEO Schulman)) while Q1 2026 emphasized turning seasonal Q1 positive ("55,000 postpaid phone net adds" and "the first time in 13 years" (Director & CEO Schulman)) alongside churn and cost-to-serve improvements.

Risks and Concerns

  • "We operate in a dynamic and rapidly changing landscape" and guidance reflected "a prudent view of competitive dynamics and the macro political and economic environment" (Director & CEO Schulman).

  • Network reliability remained a focus after the outage, including "a onetime pressure of 80 basis points" from credits (Director & CEO Schulman), with management emphasizing actions to retain customers and improve satisfaction.

  • Execution risk around cost and integration remained central, with management stating Frontier integration "is on track" and targeting "more than $1 billion of run rate operating cost synergies by 2028" (Director & CEO Schulman).

Final Takeaway

Management framed Q1 as early proof points that the turnaround and transformation program are translating into higher-quality net adds, lower churn, and improved operating leverage, while keeping full-year mobility and broadband service revenue growth guidance at 2% to 3%. Leadership raised 2026 adjusted EPS growth guidance to 5% to 6%, reiterated free cash flow guidance of $21.5 billion or more, and emphasized that lower acquisition/retention costs, Frontier integration, and an AI-led operating model are intended to support stronger cash generation and sustained capital returns, including buybacks and the dividend.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Bed Bath & Beyond aims for a connected loyalty program across brands
data.description:
Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY) announced a strategic partnership with Bilt aimed at building a more connected “everything home” experience across the Bed Bath & Beyond portfolio, which now includes Bed Bath & Beyond, The Container Store, Kirkland’s, Overstock, and buybuy BABY. The unified customer identity, loyalty, and engagement platform is designed to support customers across the full home lifecycle, from moving and furnishing to renovation and family growth.

Bilt is known as a fintech and loyalty company that helps people earn rewards on rent and other housing-related payments, then redeem those points for travel, experiences, and everyday perk

As part of the partnership, Bilt's Guest Experience Platform will act as a shared intelligence layer across the portfolio. It is designed to enable single sign-on and persistent customer profiles, modern loyalty features tied to engagement and services, more coordinated outreach and offers, and broader insight into customer behavior across brands and channels.

"Everything Home is about meeting customers where they are in life and staying relevant as their needs evolve," highlighted Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY) CEO Marcus Lemonis. "That requires a connected platform behind the scenes that works across brands, not another layer of fragmentation. Bilt gives us the ability to recognize our customers, reward long-term engagement, and build lasting relationships across the full home journey," he added.

Shares of BBBY were down 3.4% in early afternoon trading on Monday. Short interest stands at 11.4% of the total float. Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY) is scheduled to release Q1 results after the market closes.
